newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6, 2026. The notable recent catalyst - a letter of intent for ~2,700 UK reverse vending machines (deliveries mostly 2027) - dates to mid-May and is already in the price. Next earnings July 17, 2026. Day move +0.85% is noise.

sources.label: TipRanks - Tomra secures major UK reverse vending deal ahead of DRS launch
ad-hoc-news - UK deposit return order spotlight on Tomra

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since June 6 on this strict screen: the AI-chain linkage (sensor sorting recovering metals/plastics from accelerating hardware cycles) remains speculative and unproven, and a +0.85% day is pure drift. Yesterday's hold was set with the -30% YTD washout already reconciled, so nothing today justifies moving again.
verdicts.bull: Washed-out at -30% YTD with a 78% buy consensus and ~135 NOK average target, plus a fresh UK DRS rollout pipeline (~2,700 RVMs, 2027 deliveries) and ~EUR 100M of new DRS-market revenue in 2026 - asymmetry is decent if recycling demand troughs.
verdicts.neutral: The core businesses (Collection, Recycling, Food) are regulation-driven, not AI-driven; the AI-hardware-recycling angle is a thin overlay on a deposit-return story, so the name drifts with DRS rollout timing until July 17 earnings.
verdicts.bear: Recycling segment demand has collapsed and Collection revenue missed on slower DRS rollouts; if e-waste/metals recovery never materializes as an AI-cycle beneficiary, this is a low-growth industrial with no real claim to an AI-bottleneck list.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings on July 17 and conversion of the UK RVM letter of intent into a firm contract.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued recycling-demand weakness and DRS rollout delays, with the speculative AI-chain linkage failing to generate any measurable revenue.
